files.content:
Local notes workspace

NoteKeeper

Create a note

Capture text or checklist notes, organize them with labels, and keep them stored in your browser.

Checklist items

Add one or more items. You can edit text, mark items complete, and remove items before saving.

Notes

Search your saved notes by title, body text, labels, and checklist items.

No notes yet

Create your first note using the form on the left.

# NoteKeeper

NoteKeeper is a small browser-only notes app that runs directly from `index.html`. This step adds checklist note editing and pinning while keeping the app local-first and dependency-free.

## Run

1. Place these four files in the same folder:
   - `index.html`
   - `styles.css`
   - `app.js`
   - `README.md`
2. Open `index.html` directly in a modern browser.
3. No server, build tool, package manager, or external dependency is required.

## Features in this step

- Create plain text notes with an optional title and body
- Create checklist notes with multiple items
- Edit existing text and checklist notes
- Mark checklist items complete or incomplete while editing
- Remove checklist items while editing
- Delete notes permanently
- Add labels as comma-separated text
- Display labels on note cards
- Search notes by title, body, labels, and checklist item text
- Archive active notes
- Switch between active and archived note views
- Restore archived notes back to the active view
- Pin and unpin notes
- Show pinned active notes before unpinned active notes
- Visually distinguish pinned notes
- Responsive card layout
- Notes persist in `localStorage`
- Safe startup when no saved data exists
- Safe recovery to an empty list if stored JSON is malformed
- Helpful empty states for empty views and search results

## Storage

- Notes are stored in the browser under the key `notekeeper.notes`.
- Saved note data includes id, title, body, type, labels, pinned state, archived state, checklist items, and timestamps.
- Search is case-insensitive and uses simple substring matching.
- If stored JSON is missing, invalid, or malformed, NoteKeeper falls back to an empty note list.

## Manual verification

Open `index.html` and verify the following:

1. Create a plain text note with a title, body, and one or more labels.
2. Create a checklist note with multiple items.
3. Edit a checklist note and change item text.
4. Mark one checklist item complete and another incomplete, save, and confirm the states remain visible.
5. Remove a checklist item while editing and save.
6. Search by title text and confirm matching notes remain visible.
7. Search by body text and confirm matching plain text notes remain visible.
8. Search by checklist item text and confirm matching checklist notes remain visible.
9. Search by label text and confirm matching notes remain visible.
10. Pin a note and confirm it appears before unpinned notes in the active view.
11. Unpin the note and confirm it returns to normal ordering.
12. Archive a note and confirm it disappears from the active view.
13. Switch to the archived view and confirm the archived note appears there.
14. Restore the archived note and confirm it returns to the active view.
15. Delete a note and confirm it disappears.
16. Reload the page and confirm saved notes, labels, pinned state, archive state, and checklist item state remain.
17. Try a search that matches nothing and confirm the app shows a helpful empty state.
18. Tab through the form, checklist controls, search field, view buttons, and note action buttons to confirm keyboard access and visible focus states.

## Notes

- The app is intentionally small and uses plain JavaScript with `localStorage`.
- Checklist editing happens in the composer form before saving updates.
- Archived notes can still be pinned in stored data, but pinned sorting is only emphasized in the active notes view.

notes: Implemented checklist note creation/editing with persistent item state, plus pin/unpin actions and pinned-first sorting for active notes.
